ks1.objective: Carry stumps to base one at a time, then return and bowl the ball at the stumps. First team to hit the stumps wins.
successCriteria: Waits behind the cone until tagged
Carries one stump at a time

lks2.objective: Run a relay to build the base, then practice bowling at a target under team pressure.
successCriteria: Sets stumps in the ground securely
Follows the ball after each bowl

uks2.objective: Combine the relay with accurate overarm bowling under time pressure.
successCriteria: Uses overarm action with a clear delivery stride
Recovers the ball quickly to keep the relay moving

space.description: Each team starts behind a cone. A 'base' is set up opposite at throwing distance. The base starts EMPTY — no stumps in. The 3 stumps for each team start beside the starting cone.

steps: Form teams of 4. Each team lines up behind their cone.
Place 3 stumps and a ball next to the starting cone. The base opposite is empty.
On 'go', the first player picks up 1 stump, runs to the base, plants it in, and runs back to tag the next player.
Repeat until all 3 stumps are in the base.
Once the stumps are up, players take turns picking up the ball and bowling at the stumps.
After each bowl: follow the ball, collect it, return in hand to the next person.
The first team to hit the stumps wins.

errors.cause: Eager start or unclear hand-touch protocol
errors.fix: Insist on a clear tag; restart that team if missed

errors.issue: Bowling from in front of the start cone
errors.cause: Stepping past the line for power
errors.fix: Restart the bowl; chalk or mark the line clearly

errors.cause: Doesn't see retrieval as part of the team's job
errors.fix: Cue 'bowl AND fetch — your team is waiting'

progressions: Increase distance between cone and base.
Decrease distance for younger children or to keep tempo high.
questions: Which type of bowl or throw works best at this distance?
How did your team keep the ball moving quickly?
What did you do to stay accurate when you were tired?

step.space: Increase distance for older children; reduce for KS1
step.task: Switch between overarm and underarm to challenge technique
step.equipment: Use bigger / softer balls for KS1; smaller balls for UKS2
step.people: Teams of 3 or 5 also work — adjust stump count to match

base.showmanship: Build energy with a clear countdown and a loud start. Praise teams who hand the ball over cleanly and follow through after each bowl — these are the habits that win the race.
